purpose: Visual evidence for duplicate detection. Use these image files before deciding that source images are exact duplicates.
duplicateClassificationRule: Archive only images proven to be exact/same-slice/same-screenshot duplicates or unusable. Caption-only similarity is not enough; uncertain or merely similar images stay primary as recognition reinforcement.

entries.caption: Graphics depict the most common sites of axonal injury (red). Frequent but relatively less common locations are shown (green). Injury to midbrain/upper pons (purple) is uncommon but often lethal. In general, the deeper the abnormalities, the more severe the injury.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Autopsy shows hemorrhagic diffuse axonal injury (DAI). Note the linear hemorrhagic foci in the frontal lobe WM as well as the basal ganglia and external capsule . There is a hemorrhagic lesion at the junction of the frontal lobe cortex and subcortical WM .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial NECT in a 25-year-old man involved in a high-impact motor vehicle accident (MVA) shows multiple hemorrhagic foci in the subcortical WM , external and internal capsules, basal ganglia , and thalami . Blood is also present in the 3rd and lateral ventricles.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: More cephalad NECT in the same patient shows punctate peripheral hemorrhages in the subcortical WM . Traumatic subarachnoid hemorrhage (tSAH) is also present.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Sagittal graphic illustrates multiple DAI hemorrhagic foci within the corpus callosum and brainstem.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial NECT in a patient involved in a high-speed, high-impact MVA shows only diffuse brain swelling, minimal tSAH . The Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS) was 8 at the scene; by the time the patient reached the ER, the GCS had decreased to 3.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Because of the discrepancy between the lack of findings on NECT and the low GCS, axial FLAIR MR was obtained in the same patient. Note the hyperintensity in the corpus callosum and subcortical WM .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial SWI MR with MIP shows innumerable punctate and linear, hypointense foci that follow the course of the axons within the corona radiata.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial DWI MR in the same patient shows intense restriction in the frontal WM as well as throughout the corpus callosum , both fornices , and subcortical WM .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Midline sagittal DWI MR in the same patient shows the intense restricted diffusion throughout the corpus callosum . A smaller focus of restricted diffusion is present in the midbrain .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Graphics depict the most common sites of axonal injury (red). Frequent, but relatively less common locations are shown (green). Injury to midbrain/upper pons (purple) is uncommon but often lethal. In general, the deeper the abnormalities, the more severe the injury.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Autopsy shows hemorrhagic diffuse axonal injury (DAI). Note the linear hemorrhagic foci in the frontal lobe WM as well as the basal ganglia and external capsule . There is a hemorrhagic lesion at the junction of the frontal lobe cortex and subcortical WM .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: A 21-year-old female unrestrained driver, following MVA at highway speeds, is shown. Axial noncontrast CT demonstrates mild right sylvian subarachnoid hemorrhage (SAH) and small right inferior frontal parenchymal hemorrhage .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial MIP SWI MR in the same patient after decreased Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS) shows multiple foci of parenchymal susceptibility or microhemorrhage at the gray-white interfaces , characteristic of DAI. Note mild right sylvian SAH .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Sagittal graphic illustrates multiple DAI hemorrhagic foci within the corpus callosum and brainstem.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial NECT in a patient involved in a high-speed, high-impact MVA shows only diffuse brain swelling, minimal traumatic SAH (tSAH) . The GCS was 8 at the scene. By the time the patient reached the ER, the GCS had decreased to 3.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Because of the discrepancy between the lack of findings on NECT and the low GCS, axial FLAIR MR was obtained in the same patient. Note the edema in the corpus callosum and subcortical WM .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial SWI MR in the same patient with MIP shows innumerable punctate and linear, hypointense foci that follow the course of the axons within the corona radiata. DAI is best detected by FLAIR for nonhemorrhagic and SWI for hemorrhagic lesions.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial DWI MR in the same patient shows intense restriction in the frontal WM as well as throughout the corpus callosum , both fornices , and subcortical WM .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Midline sagittal DWI MR in the same patient shows the intense restricted diffusion throughout the corpus callosum . A smaller focus of restricted diffusion is present in the midbrain .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial T2 MR in the same patient (obtained immediately following the CT because of the gross discrepancy between clinical and imaging findings) shows hyperintensities in the right thalamus and WM of both frontal lobes . A torn arachnoid is probably responsible for the small bifrontal subdural hygromas .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial SWI MR in the same patient shows innumerable linear and ovoid blooming hypointensities in the subcortical and deep WM , consistent with diffuse vascular injury (severe DAI).
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial NECT in a patient who was in a high-impact MVA with GCS = 5 shows some intraventricular hemorrhage and subdural hygromas , probably a torn arachnoid. Minimal tSAH is present.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: More cephalad NECT in the same patient shows tSAH and trace subdural hemorrhage along the falx .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial T2 MR in the same patient shows the subdural hygromas .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial FLAIR MR shows some hyperintensities in the subcortical and deep WM along with tSAH .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial T2* GRE MR shows linear hemorrhages in the subcortical WM and corpus callosum .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial T2* SWI MR with MIP shows many more linear and punctate hemorrhages in the subcortical and deep periventricular WM as well as in the basal ganglia , corpus callosum , and fornices .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: More cephalad T2* SWI MR shows the WM hemorrhages "end on" as multifocal dot-like foci of susceptibility .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial DWI MR shows restricted diffusion in the fornices as well as scattered foci in the subcortical and deep WM.
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Microscopic postmortem study of WM in severe DAI shows axonal "retraction balls" and numerous empty "holes" in the brain parenchyma.

entries.caption: Axial T2* GRE MR shows DAI manifested as multiple bifrontal foci of susceptibility .
entries.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

entries.caption: Axial FLAIR MR shows hyperintense edema of the corpus callosum with central hypointense hemorrhage and edema in the left parietal subcortical WM .

entries.caption: Axial NECT shows focal hemorrhage in the splenium of the corpus callosum with intraventricular spread .

entries.caption: Axial NECT in the same patient reveals an additional focus of hemorrhage in the subcortical WM of the frontal lobe .

entries.caption: Axial T2* GRE MR shows focal, hypointense hemorrhage in the posterolateral mesencephalon and additional microbleeds in the subcortical WM of the temporal and frontal lobes .

entries.caption: Axial T2 MR at 3.0T shows normal findings in a patient with severe posttraumatic headache and difficulty concentrating. Head CT was also normal (not shown).

entries.caption: Axial T2* GRE MR at 3.0T in the same patient shows foci of hypointensity in the frontal WM due to microbleeds .

entries.caption: Axial T2* GRE MR shows multifocal hypointense foci from susceptibility of hemorrhagic DAI. Most lesions are at the gray matter-white matter interface, but some are also periventricular.

entries.caption: Axial T2* GRE MR shows multifocal hypointense foci from susceptibility of hemorrhagic DAI. Deep WM and pericallosal tissue are involved.

entries.caption: Axial NECT demonstrates multiple petechial hemorrhages at the bifrontal gray matter-white matter interfaces in this patient with DAI. Note the presence of SAH .

entries.caption: Axial NECT shows hyperdense hemorrhage within the fornices from DAI. The splenium is hypodense from nonhemorrhagic DAI involvement.

entries.caption: Axial DWI MR shows restricted diffusion with susceptibility within the fornices from hemorrhage. The splenium is hyperintense from nonhemorrhagic DAI.

entries.caption: A 43-year-old man s/p motorcycle accident with GCS 3 is shown. Axial FLAIR shows edema and hemorrhage in the posterior midbrain , consistent with DAI.
